title: Schema dedupe (C10) — drop auditMode in favor of audit_mode (snake_case parity with sibling keys)
description: Delete the camelCase `auditMode` duplicate from fabric-config zod schema (L52) and FabricConfig TS type (L32), keeping only the snake_case `audit_mode` (L53/L33). Sibling keys (archive_hint_hours, review_hint_pending_count, import_window_first_run_months, fabric_language) all use snake_case — this dedupe restores convention parity. Zero runtime consumers read either spelling (only test fixtures). Update 3 test files: fabric-config.test.ts (L108 fixture + L117 assertion), schemas-roundtrip.test.ts (L52 input field), zod-roundtrip.test.ts (L44-L62 fast-check arbitrary, delete L49-L53 auditMode block, keep L54-L58 audit_mode block). Per memory feedback_clean_slate.md: NO zod preprocess shim, no 'legacy auditMode detected' warning — direct removal only. Docs at docs/configuration.md:258 already canonical on snake_case (no docs update). Isolated to packages/shared — orthogonal to all CLI changes. Commit: `refactor(rc15): schema dedupe — drop auditMode in favor of audit_mode (snake_case parity)`

files.change: Delete L52: `auditMode: auditModeSchema.optional(),`. Keep L53: `audit_mode: auditModeSchema.optional(),` as sole spelling. Schema remains non-strict (no .strict() call) — preserves forward-compat for future config additions per exploration constraint.
Delete L32: `auditMode?: AuditMode;`. Keep L33: `audit_mode?: AuditMode;` as sole TS mirror.

Update L108 inside previousVersionFixture: `auditMode: 'strict' as const,` → `audit_mode: 'strict' as const,`. Update L117 assertion: `expect(parsed.auditMode).toBe('strict')` → `expect(parsed.audit_mode).toBe('strict')`. Without these updates, parse silently succeeds (non-strict schema drops the unknown key) but parsed.auditMode becomes undefined and the assertion fails.

files.target: L47-L55 (I1.1 fabricConfigSchema round-trip — full config with all fields)
files.change: Update L52: `auditMode: 'strict'` → `audit_mode: 'strict'`. Round-trip identity assertion now holds (input.audit_mode === parsed.audit_mode).

files.target: L44-L62 (fabricConfigArbitrary fast-check generator), L49-L53 (auditMode block — DELETE), L54-L58 (audit_mode block — KEEP)
files.change: Delete L49-L53 (auditMode constantFrom fc arbitrary block); keep L54-L58 (audit_mode block) as the sole audit-related field generator. The arbitrary now produces configs with only the canonical snake_case spelling.

implementation: Read packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts L40-L60 to confirm L52/L53 line positions and surrounding context
Edit fabric-config.ts: delete the L52 `auditMode: auditModeSchema.optional(),` line
Read packages/shared/src/types/config.ts L25-L40 to confirm L32/L33 positions
Edit types/config.ts: delete the L32 `auditMode?: AuditMode;` line
Edit packages/shared/test/fabric-config.test.ts: change L108 from `auditMode: 'strict' as const,` to `audit_mode: 'strict' as const,`; change L117 assertion field name

Edit packages/shared/test/property-based/zod-roundtrip.test.ts L44-L62: delete the L49-L53 auditMode fast-check arbitrary block; verify L54-L58 audit_mode block remains and the wrapping fc.record continues to type-check
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared test` — verify all schema tests pass

Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared typecheck` — verify FabricConfig type users (if any in CLI) still compile (expectation: zero runtime consumers per exploration; CLI/server don't read either spelling)
Run `pnpm test` at root — verify no other packages reference auditMode (search via grep — only test fixtures should match)
Run `pnpm lint` — verify no lint errors
git add + commit with message: `refactor(rc15): schema dedupe — drop auditMode in favor of audit_mode (snake_case parity)`

reference.pattern: Schema dedupe pattern: when zod is non-strict, unknown keys silently drop on parse. Tests that asserted on the old spelling will fail at assertion time, not parse time — the post-dedupe failure mode is `undefined !== 'strict'` mismatch. Fix: rename test field name to match the canonical schema key.
reference.examples: Sibling snake_case keys for convention reference: archive_hint_hours, review_hint_pending_count, import_window_first_run_months, fabric_language. See exploration-schema-and-test-impact.json Pattern 1 (auditMode rename, scoped, mechanical).

criteria: packages/shared/src/types/config.ts contains only `audit_mode?: AuditMode;` — zero `auditMode` references
Zero `auditMode` references in packages/shared (grep across src/ and test/)
Zero `auditMode` references in packages/cli and packages/server (grep — exploration confirmed zero runtime consumers)
packages/shared/test/fabric-config.test.ts uses `audit_mode: 'strict' as const` in previousVersionFixture and `parsed.audit_mode` in assertions
`pnpm test` (root) exit 0 — no other test breaks

rationale.chosen_approach: Direct removal (no preprocess shim, no deprecation warning) per memory feedback_clean_slate.md and zero-user state. The schema is non-strict so dropping the duplicate field doesn't break existing configs at parse time — they silently lose the camelCase setting. Test fixtures get rename-only updates.
alternatives_considered: Add zod.preprocess to rewrite auditMode→audit_mode for backward-compat
Mark auditMode as @deprecated in TS type for one more RC
Add a runtime warning log when auditMode is detected in parsed config
decision_factors: Zero runtime consumers (exploration verified — only test fixtures read either spelling)
Convention parity with sibling snake_case keys (archive_hint_hours, fabric_language, etc.)
Docs at configuration.md:258 already canonical on snake_case — code catches up to docs
rationale.tradeoffs: Any external config file in the wild carrying camelCase auditMode will silently drop the setting at parse time. Acceptable given zero published users. Future strict-mode adoption could surface these as parse errors but is out of scope.

manual_checks: Write a temp fabric-config.json with `"audit_mode": "strict"` and verify it parses correctly (parsed.audit_mode === 'strict')
Write a temp fabric-config.json with `"auditMode": "strict"` and verify it parses (no error — non-strict schema) but parsed.audit_mode is undefined (the dropped-on-parse semantics)
success_metrics: All shared package tests pass: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared test` exit 0
